stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance in the ML community.
The associated tags point to its use in text-generation pipelines using transformers.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the highly competitive and significant large language model landscape.

stories.title: Meta’s own AI was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Meta's AI support chatbot was exploited by hackers to compromise Instagram accounts.
The attack involved tricking the chatbot into changing the associated email address for a target profile.
This vulnerability highlights significant security risks within large-scale AI customer service implementations.

stories.title: Anthropic has officially filed to go public
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Anthropic has officially filed with the SEC to begin its Initial Public Offering (IPO).
This filing marks a significant development in the competitive landscape between major AI labs like Anthropic and OpenAI.
The IPO is anticipated to be a major financial event given Anthropic's recent fundraising success.

stories.title: Dozens of Red Hat packages backdoored through its official NPM channel
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dozens of Red Hat packages were found to be backdoored.
The compromise occurred through Red Hat's official NPM channel.
Users who downloaded affected packages are urged to investigate immediately.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST signals a push towards open-source, safety-focused LLM deployment.

stories.title: This could be Windows’ M1 moment — but expect it to cost a ton
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Nvidia is entering the consumer laptop chip market with its RTX Spark technology.
The announcement signals a potential shift in the Windows ecosystem, mirroring Apple's success with M-series chips.
The article notes that while Arm chips show promise, Windows performance with current Qualcomm chips has lagged.

stories.title: Hackers duped Meta AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Hackers exploited a vulnerability in Meta's AI support chatbot.
The exploit allowed unauthorized access to and theft of celebrity Instagram accounts.
The stolen accounts were subsequently resold before Meta could patch the security flaw.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI, Sam Altman after multiple ChatGPT-linked murders
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Florida is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman following multiple alleged murders linked to ChatGPT.
The lawsuit alleges that Sam Altman demonstrates an 'utter disregard' for human lives.
This represents a significant legal and ethical challenge regarding the deployment and safety of large language models.

stories.title: From 15 hours to one minute: How AI/ML is speeding up GM's development
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: General Motors (GM) is leveraging AI/ML to drastically accelerate vehicle development timelines.
The shift involves heavy virtualization, utilizing technologies like CFD (Computational Fluid Dynamics) and FEA (Finite Element Analysis).
The integration of digital twins is a key component in this accelerated, data-driven manufacturing process.

stories.title: PolySpeech-100: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Speech Understanding Across 100+ Languages and Dialects
stories.author: You He
bullet_points: Introduces PolySpeech-100, a large-scale benchmark for speech understanding across over 100 languages and dialects.
Addresses critical limitations in current Speech-LLM evaluation, specifically bias towards high-resource languages and focus on ASR over semantic reasoning.
Aims to push the evaluation of End-to-End Speech-LLMs beyond simple transcription to assess native-level understanding.

stories.title: Lost in Delusion: Examining LLM Safety Under User Delusions and Distress
stories.author: Maarten Sap
bullet_points: Investigates LLM safety when users exhibit psychological distress intertwined with delusional beliefs.
Addresses a gap in current research by simulating sustained, multi-turn conversations with clinically grounded personas.
Evaluates the behavior of six different LLMs under complex, high-stakes mental health scenarios.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating its generative capabilities.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating with popular local LLM runners.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance Claude Code's real-time knowledge access.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API access.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Gemini’s new AI agent is about as good as Google’s demo
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new AI agent called Gemini Spark, marketed as a '24/7' assistant.
The agent demonstrates surprisingly capable performance in task execution, according to the review.
The analyst raises concerns regarding the financial cost and potential privacy implications of using Spark.

stories.title: AI costs how much? GitHub Copilot users react to new usage-based pricing system.
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: GitHub Copilot is implementing a new usage-based pricing model for its AI features.
Early user reactions indicate that high usage can quickly deplete monthly credit allotments.
The article addresses the practical financial implications of adopting AI coding assistants.

stories.title: Moderna gets $50 million to develop mRNA Ebola vaccine against Bundibugyo
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Moderna is receiving $50 million in funding.
The funding is specifically for developing an mRNA Ebola vaccine.
The vaccine targets the Bundibugyo strain amid an ongoing Ebola outbreak.

stories.title: Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra looks like its first true MacBook Pro competitor
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft has released the Surface Laptop Ultra, positioning it as a serious competitor to the MacBook Pro.
The device is described as Microsoft's most refined effort in the high-end mobile workstation category.
The announcement signals Microsoft's increased focus on challenging Apple's premium hardware segment.

stories.title: Revise, Don't Freeze: Sampler-Matched Training for Self-Correcting Masked Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Greg Ver Steeg
bullet_points: Introduces D3IM, a new parameter-free sampler designed for Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs).
Addresses a limitation where standard samplers commit tokens too early, preventing the model from utilizing its inherent revision capability.
Proposes a principled method to directly revise already visible tokens without needing auxiliary or complex remasking modules.

stories.title: DSL-LLaDA: Scaling Continuous Denoising to 8B Masked Diffusion LMs
stories.author: Greg Ver Steeg
bullet_points: Introduces DSL-LLaDA, a method to scale Continuous Denoising to 8B Masked Diffusion Language Models (LMs).
Addresses the quality vs. length tradeoff inherent in few-step decoding of discrete masked diffusion models.
Proposes using continuous denoising in embedding space to jointly evolve all positions, overcoming previous scaling challenges.

stories.title: Hybrid Verified Decoding: Learning to Allocate Verification in Speculative Decoding
stories.author: Phillip Howard
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of autoregressive LLM decoding.
Proposes 'Hybrid Verified Decoding' to improve speculative decoding efficiency.
Focuses on intelligently allocating verification steps to maximize speedup, especially in structured/agentic workloads.

stories.title: Trust Functions: Near-Lossless Weak-to-Strong Generalization by Learning When to Trust the Weak Teacher
stories.author: Daniel Khashabi
bullet_points: The research addresses weak-to-strong generalization, a method for improving strong models using supervision from weaker teachers when true labels are scarce.
The core contribution is the introduction of 'trust functions' that assign a scalar trust score to each weak label.
These trust scores are used to filter weak supervision, effectively framing the problem as a data selection challenge.

stories.title: Robust Asynchronous Planning via Auto-Formalization
stories.author: Li Zhang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of asynchronous planning in LLM-based systems, which is crucial for real-world tasks.
It unifies asynchronous planning challenges under a single formulation, distinguishing between direct 'Planner' generation and external 'Formalizer' translation.
The authors introduce three new benchmarks designed to test these complex, real-world asynchronous planning scenarios at scale.

stories.title: HypothesisMed: Inference-Time Answer Fusion and Structured Hypothesis-Space Reporting for Biomedical Question Answering
stories.author: Ge Wang
bullet_points: HypothesisMed introduces an inference-time reliability pipeline for biomedical multiple-choice question answering using LLMs.
It addresses limitations of standard answer accuracy metrics by focusing on structured output, weak answer space recognition, and avoiding confident errors.
The method combines direct, chain-of-thought, and a specialized HypothesisMed-v3 prompting approach.

stories.title: The newest Instagram “exploit” is the goofiest I've seen
stories.author: ssiddharth
bullet_points: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support bot to compromise Instagram accounts.
The exploit leverages vulnerabilities within the AI support system, suggesting a new attack vector.
The story is reported on Hacker News, indicating moderate interest within the tech community.

stories.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
stories.author: typpo
bullet_points: OpenAI's frontier models are now accessible via AWS.
Codex, OpenAI's code generation model, is also available on the AWS platform.
This integration signifies a major shift in cloud infrastructure for accessing cutting-edge AI.

stories.title: A new way to build chips: Sequentially stacking silicon to extend Moore's Law
stories.author: hhs
bullet_points: The story discusses a novel chip manufacturing technique.
The method involves sequentially stacking silicon layers.
The goal of this technique is to extend the viability of Moore's Law.

stories.title: Claude Code Codex 마이그레이션 가이드 2026 — 7단계 절차·도구 비교
stories.author: treesoop
bullet_points: Provides a 7-step migration guide from Claude Code to Codex.
Details automatic conversion processes and how to replace Skills/Hooks during the transition.
Offers practical insights, including tool comparisons, hybrid patterns, and common failure points verified by AI-Native teams.
